flashcards.back: P(H|D) = [P(D|H) × P(H)] / P(D), where H is hypothesis and D is data

flashcards.front: What is the difference between prior and posterior probability?
flashcards.back: Prior is the probability before seeing data, posterior is the probability after incorporating evidence

flashcards.front: What is the naïve assumption in Naïve Bayes?
flashcards.back: All features are conditionally independent given the class label

flashcards.front: Name one advantage of Bayesian learning
flashcards.back: Incorporates prior knowledge/Quantifies uncertainty/Handles sequential learning/Avoids overfitting

flashcards.front: How does Bayesian approach differ from frequentist approach?
flashcards.back: Bayesian treats parameters as random variables, frequentist treats them as fixed unknown quantities
